data.narrative:
SatRev is a Series B-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Grzegorz Zwoli­ński. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 private peers at identical stage and sector (Aalyria, Hubble Network, Sift) alongside a broader reference set of 5 public Space & Aerospace names (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

The peer-median method is the sole applicable triangulation approach here. Three same-sector, same-stage private companies traded at a median valuation of $400M, with a range spanning $274M (Sift) to $1.3B (Aalyria). This dispersion reflects meaningful heterogeneity in the Space & Aerospace segment at Series B. Public-comp anchoring is not viable; the sector median P/S of 53.2x across 6 tickers cannot be applied without SatRev's revenue figure, which is not disclosed.

The single-method analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, centered on $400M. This range captures reasonable positioning within the peer cohort but carries material uncertainty given the small sample size and the 375% spread between floor and ceiling in the underlying peer set. Key unknowns include SatRev's revenue trajectory, unit economics, and market share relative to Aalyria and Hubble Network. Any valuation outside the $340M–$460M band would require evidence of differentiation materially stronger or weaker than the median peer profile.
